comments.body: You should probably try with thenCallRealMethod globally and actually mock the method in this one test. Read more here: javadoc.io/static/org.mockito/mockito-core/3.4.6/org/mockito‌​/…
Why not write the testErrorHandlingBranch method in a separate Test class?
Thanks, but the PermissionBO is not returned from userBO. I'll clarify my question.
I don't understand exactly what do you need to do. If PermissionBO is at the same level as UserBO than probably you need to mock also PermissionBO.
Thanks! I was completely wrong. There is no reason why PermissionBO was not yet mocked completely in this class!
This is not the right answer. This wont make it work perfectly.

answers.body:
You should use thenReturn() inside your test method in the way to use a specific mocked value only inside a specific test.

For example: Mockito.when(userBO.example()).thenReturn("Specific test method mock");

@MockBean at the field level applies the mock to the Spring ApplicationContext for all the tests in that class. You can't add or remove it for a single method.

The idiomatic solution is to use a nested test class. This allows you to create a distinct testing configuration (with its own set of mocks) for a specific group of tests.

import org.junit.jupiter.api.Nested;
import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test;
import org.springframework.beans.factory.annotation.Autowired;
import org.springframework.boot.test.autoconfigure.web.servlet.AutoConfigureMockMvc;
import org.springframework.boot.test.context.SpringBootTest;
import org.springframework.boot.test.mock.mockito.MockBean;
import org.springframework.test.web.servlet.MockMvc;

import static org.junit.jupiter.api.Assertions.assertThrows;
import static org.mockito.BDDMockito.given;
import static org.springframework.test.web.servlet.request.MockMvcRequestBuilders.get;
import static org.springframework.test.web.servlet.result.MockMvcResultMatchers.status;

@SpringBootTest
@AutoConfigureMockMvc
public class UserTest {

    @Autowired
    private MockMvc mockMvc;

    @MockBean
    private UserBO userBO;

    @Test
    void testRegularUserFlow() throws Exception {
        given(userBO.getUser(1L)).willReturn(new User("Normal User"));

        mockMvc.perform(get("/users/1"))
                .andExpect(status().isOk());
    }

    @Nested
    class ErrorHandlingTest {

        @MockBean
        private PermissionsBO permissionsBO;

        @MockBean
        private UserBO userBO; // Re-declared from outer class

        @Test
        void testErrorHandlingBranch() throws Exception {
            given(permissionsBO.validateAction()).willThrow(new IllegalStateException("Permission Denied!"));
            
            given(userBO.getUser(1L)).willReturn(new User("Admin User"));
            
            mockMvc.perform(get("/users/1/do-admin-action"))
                   .andExpect(status().isInternalServerError());
            
            });
        }
    }
}

class User { public User(String name) {} }
interface UserBO { User getUser(long id); }
interface PermissionsBO { boolean validateAction(); }

title: Can I use @MockBean to mock a bean in just one method of my test class?
body:
I have this mvc test class

@RunWith(SpringRunner.class)
@SpringBootTest
public class UserTest {

    private MockMvc mockMvc;

    @Resource
    private WebApplicationContext webApplicationContext;

    @MockBean
    private UserBO userBO;

    @Before
    public void init() {
        mockMvc = MockMvcBuilders.webAppContextSetup(webApplicationContext).build();
// ...
    }
// ...

In one single test case I need to mock another bean to cover a special error handling branch. Is this possible? Is there any method I can use which does the same as @MockBean for the UserBO? (PermissionBO is not retuned by UserBO but used on the same level as UserBO in the class under test.)

@Test(expects=IllegalStatusException.class)
public void testErrorHandlingBranch() {
    // How can I mock the bean *PermissionsBO* only for this test?
    // like @MockBean PermissionsBO permissionsBO;
    // Is there a method like injectMockBean(PermissionsBO.class)?
    mockMvc.perform(...)
}
